steps.title: Square the equation
steps.content:
Start from the original equation and square both sides.
$$\sqrt{k-x}=53-x$$
$$k-x=(53-x)^2$$
$$x^2-105x+(2{,}809-k)=0$$

steps.title: Use the single solution boundary
steps.content:
For the squared quadratic to have exactly one real solution, set the discriminant equal to $0$.
$$105^2-4(2{,}809-k)=0$$
$$11{,}025-11{,}236+4k=0$$
$$4k=211$$

steps.title: Check the original equation
steps.content: At the boundary, the repeated root is $x=52.5$, and $53-x$ is positive, so it satisfies the original square-root equation. The answer is 211.
